   176→	} else {
   177→		// Fall back to session state (e.g., auto-commit strategy updates it after each save)
   178→		sessionState, loadErr := strategy.LoadSessionState(sessionID)
   179→		if loadErr != nil {
   180→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load session state: %v\n", loadErr)
   181→		}
   182→		if sessionState != nil && sessionState.CheckpointTranscriptStart > 0 {
   183→			transcriptOffset = sessionState.CheckpointTranscriptStart
   184→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Session state found: parsing transcript from line %d\n", transcriptOffset)
   185→		}
   186→	}
   187→
   188→	// Parse transcript (optionally from offset for strategies that track transcript position)
   189→	// When transcriptOffset > 0, only parse NEW lines since the last checkpoint
   190→	var transcriptLines []transcriptLine
   191→	var totalLines int
   192→	if transcriptOffset > 0 {
   193→		// Parse only NEW lines since last checkpoint
   194→		transcriptLines, totalLines, err = transcript.ParseFromFileAtLine(transcriptPath, transcriptOffset)
   195→		if err != nil {
   196→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse transcript from line %d: %w", transcriptOffset, err)
   197→		}
   198→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Parsed %d new transcript lines (total: %d)\n", len(transcriptLines), totalLines)
   199→	} else {
   200→		// First prompt or no session state - parse entire transcript
   201→		// Use ParseFromFileAtLine with offset 0 to also get totalLines
   202→		transcriptLines, totalLines, err = transcript.ParseFromFileAtLine(transcriptPath, 0)
   203→		if err != nil {
   204→			return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse transcript: %w", err)
   205→		}
   206→	}
   207→
   208→	// Extract all prompts since last checkpoint for prompt file
   209→	allPrompts := extractUserPrompts(transcriptLines)
   210→	promptFile := filepath.Join(sessionDirAbs, paths.PromptFileName)
   211→	promptContent := strings.Join(allPrompts, "\n\n---\n\n")
   212→	if err := os.WriteFile(promptFile, []byte(promptContent), 0o600); err != nil {
   213→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write prompt file: %w", err)
   214→	}
   215→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Extracted %d prompt(s) to: %s\n", len(allPrompts), sessionDir+"/"+paths.PromptFileName)
   216→
   217→	// Extract summary
   218→	summaryFile := filepath.Join(sessionDirAbs, paths.SummaryFileName)
   219→	summary := extractLastAssistantMessage(transcriptLines)
   220→	if err := os.WriteFile(summaryFile, []byte(summary), 0o600); err != nil {
   221→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to write summary file: %w", err)
   222→	}
   223→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Extracted summary to: %s\n", sessionDir+"/"+paths.SummaryFileName)
   224→
   225→	// Get modified files from transcript (main agent + subagents).
   226→	// Subagent transcripts live in <transcriptDir>/<modelSessionID>/subagents/
   227→	subagentsDir := filepath.Join(filepath.Dir(transcriptPath), input.SessionID, "subagents")
   228→	modifiedFiles, subagentErr := claudecode.ExtractAllModifiedFiles(transcriptPath, transcriptOffset, subagentsDir)
   229→	if subagentErr != nil {
   230→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to extract modified files with subagents: %v\n", subagentErr)
   231→		// Fall back to main transcript only
   232→		modifiedFiles = extractModifiedFiles(transcriptLines)
   233→	}
   234→
   235→	// Generate commit message from last user prompt
   236→	lastPrompt := ""
   237→	if len(allPrompts) > 0 {
   238→		lastPrompt = allPrompts[len(allPrompts)-1]
   239→	}
   240→	commitMessage := generateCommitMessage(lastPrompt)
   241→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Using commit message: %s\n", commitMessage)
   242→
   243→	// Get repo root for path conversion (not cwd, since Claude may be in a subdirectory)
   244→	// Using cwd would filter out files in sibling directories (paths starting with ..)
   245→	repoRoot, err := paths.RepoRoot()
   246→	if err != nil {
   247→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get repo root: %w", err)
   248→	}
   249→
   250→	if preState != nil {
   251→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Pre-prompt state: %d pre-existing untracked files\n", len(preState.UntrackedFiles))
   252→	}
   253→
   254→	// Compute new and deleted files (single git status call)
   255→	changes, err := DetectFileChanges(preState.PreUntrackedFiles())
   256→	if err != nil {
   257→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to compute file changes: %v\n", err)
   258→	}
   259→
   260→	// Filter and normalize all paths (CLI responsibility)
   261→	relModifiedFiles := FilterAndNormalizePaths(modifiedFiles, repoRoot)
   262→	var relNewFiles, relDeletedFiles []string
   263→	if changes != nil {
   264→		relNewFiles = FilterAndNormalizePaths(changes.New, repoRoot)
   265→		relDeletedFiles = FilterAndNormalizePaths(changes.Deleted, repoRoot)
   266→	}
   267→
   268→	// Check if there are any changes to commit
   269→	totalChanges := len(relModifiedFiles) + len(relNewFiles) + len(relDeletedFiles)
   270→	if totalChanges == 0 {
   271→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "No files were modified during this session\n")
   272→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Skipping commit\n")
   273→		// Still transition phase even when skipping commit — the turn is ending.
   274→		transitionSessionTurnEnd(sessionID)
   275→		// Clean up state even when skipping
   276→		if err := CleanupPrePromptState(sessionID); err != nil {
   277→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to cleanup pre-prompt state: %v\n", err)
   278→		}
   279→		return nil
   280→	}
   281→
   282→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Files modified during session (%d):\n", len(relModifiedFiles))
   283→	for _, file := range relModifiedFiles {
   284→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  - %s\n", file)
   285→	}
   286→	if len(relNewFiles) > 0 {
   287→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "New files created (%d):\n", len(relNewFiles))
   288→		for _, file := range relNewFiles {
   289→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  + %s\n", file)
   290→		}
   291→	}
   292→	if len(relDeletedFiles) > 0 {
   293→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Files deleted (%d):\n", len(relDeletedFiles))
   294→		for _, file := range relDeletedFiles {
   295→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "  - %s\n", file)
   296→		}
   297→	}
   298→
   299→	// Create context file before saving changes
   300→	contextFile := filepath.Join(sessionDirAbs, paths.ContextFileName)
   301→	if err := createContextFileMinimal(contextFile, commitMessage, sessionID, promptFile, summaryFile, transcriptLines); err != nil {
   302→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create context file: %w", err)
   303→	}
   304→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Created context file: %s\n", sessionDir+"/"+paths.ContextFileName)
   305→
   306→	// Get git author from local/global config
   307→	author, err := GetGitAuthor()
   308→	if err != nil {
   309→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get git author: %w", err)
   310→	}
   311→
   312→	// Get the configured strategy
   313→	strat := GetStrategy()
   314→
   315→	// Get agent type from the currently executing hook agent (authoritative source)
   316→	var agentType agent.AgentType
   317→	if hookAgent, agentErr := GetCurrentHookAgent(); agentErr == nil {
   318→		agentType = hookAgent.Type()
   319→	}
   320→
   321→	// Get transcript position from pre-prompt state (captured at step/turn start)
   322→	var transcriptIdentifierAtStart string
   323→	var transcriptLinesAtStart int
   324→	if preState != nil {
   325→		transcriptIdentifierAtStart = preState.LastTranscriptIdentifier
   326→		transcriptLinesAtStart = preState.StepTranscriptStart
   327→	}
   328→
   329→	// Calculate token usage for this checkpoint (Claude Code specific)
   330→	var tokenUsage *agent.TokenUsage
   331→	if transcriptPath != "" {
   332→		usage, tokenErr := claudecode.CalculateTotalTokenUsage(transcriptPath, transcriptLinesAtStart, subagentsDir)
   333→		if tokenErr != nil {
   334→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to calculate token usage: %v\n", tokenErr)
   335→		} else {
   336→			tokenUsage = usage
   337→		}
   338→	}
   339→
   340→	// Build fully-populated save context and delegate to strategy
   341→	ctx := strategy.SaveContext{
   342→		SessionID:                sessionID,
   343→		ModifiedFiles:            relModifiedFiles,
   344→		NewFiles:                 relNewFiles,
   345→		DeletedFiles:             relDeletedFiles,
   346→		MetadataDir:              sessionDir,
   347→		MetadataDirAbs:           sessionDirAbs,
   348→		CommitMessage:            commitMessage,
   349→		TranscriptPath:           transcriptPath,
   350→		AuthorName:               author.Name,
   351→		AuthorEmail:              author.Email,
   352→		AgentType:                agentType,
   353→		StepTranscriptIdentifier: transcriptIdentifierAtStart,
   354→		StepTranscriptStart:      transcriptLinesAtStart,
   355→		TokenUsage:               tokenUsage,
   356→	}
   357→
   358→	if err := strat.SaveChanges(ctx); err != nil {
   359→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save changes: %w", err)
   360→	}
   361→
   362→	// Update session state with new transcript position for strategies that create
   363→	// commits on the active branch (auto-commit strategy). This prevents parsing old transcript
   364→	// lines on subsequent checkpoints.
   365→	// Note: Shadow strategy tracks transcript position per-step via StepTranscriptStart in
   366→	// pre-prompt state, but doesn't advance CheckpointTranscriptStart in session state because
   367→	// its checkpoints accumulate all files touched across the entire session.
   368→	if strat.Name() == strategy.StrategyNameAutoCommit {
   369→		// Load session state for updating transcript position
   370→		sessionState, loadErr := strategy.LoadSessionState(sessionID)
   371→		if loadErr != nil {
   372→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load session state: %v\n", loadErr)
   373→		}
   374→		// Create session state lazily if it doesn't exist (backward compat for resumed sessions
   375→		// or if InitializeSession was never called/failed)
   376→		if sessionState == nil {
   377→			sessionState = &strategy.SessionState{
   378→				SessionID: sessionID,
   379→			}
   380→		}
   381→		sessionState.CheckpointTranscriptStart = totalLines
   382→		sessionState.StepCount++
   383→		if updateErr := strategy.SaveSessionState(sessionState); updateErr != nil {
   384→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to update session state: %v\n", updateErr)
   385→		} else {
   386→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Updated session state: transcript position=%d, checkpoint=%d\n",
   387→				totalLines, sessionState.StepCount)
   388→		}
   389→	}
   390→
   391→	// Fire EventTurnEnd to transition session phase (all strategies).
   392→	// This moves ACTIVE → IDLE.
   393→	transitionSessionTurnEnd(sessionID)
   394→
   395→	// Clean up pre-prompt state (CLI responsibility)
   396→	if err := CleanupPrePromptState(sessionID); err != nil {
   397→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to cleanup pre-prompt state: %v\n", err)
   398→	}
   399→
   400→	return nil
   401→}
   402→
   403→// handleClaudeCodePostTodo handles the PostToolUse[TodoWrite] hook for subagent checkpoints.
   404→// Creates a checkpoint if we're in a subagent context (active pre-task file exists).
   405→// Skips silently if not in subagent context (main agent).
   406→func handleClaudeCodePostTodo() error {
   407→	input, err := parseSubagentCheckpointHookInput(os.Stdin)
   408→	if err != nil {
   409→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse PostToolUse[TodoWrite] input: %w", err)
   410→	}
   411→
   412→	// Get agent for logging context
   413→	ag, err := GetCurrentHookAgent()
   414→	if err != nil {
   415→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", err)
   416→	}
   417→
   418→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
   419→	logging.Info(logCtx, "post-todo",
   420→		slog.String("hook", "post-todo"),
   421→		slog.String("hook_type", "subagent"),
   422→		slog.String("model_session_id", input.SessionID),
   423→		slog.String("transcript_path", input.TranscriptPath),
   424→		slog.String("tool_use_id", input.ToolUseID),
   425→	)
   426→
   427→	// Check if we're in a subagent context by looking for an active pre-task file
   428→	taskToolUseID, found := FindActivePreTaskFile()
   429→	if !found {
   430→		// Not in subagent context - this is a main agent TodoWrite, skip
   431→		return nil
   432→	}
   433→
   434→	// Skip on default branch to avoid polluting main/master history
   435→	if skip, branchName := ShouldSkipOnDefaultBranch(); skip {
   436→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Entire: skipping incremental checkpoint on branch '%s'\n", branchName)
   437→		return nil
   438→	}
   439→
   440→	// Detect file changes since last checkpoint
   441→	changes, err := DetectFileChanges(nil)
   442→	if err != nil {
   443→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to detect changed files: %v\n", err)
   444→		return nil
   445→	}
   446→
   447→	// If no file changes, skip creating a checkpoint
   448→	if len(changes.Modified) == 0 && len(changes.New) == 0 && len(changes.Deleted) == 0 {
   449→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "[entire] No file changes detected, skipping incremental checkpoint\n")
   450→		return nil
   451→	}
   452→
   453→	// Get git author
   454→	author, err := GetGitAuthor()
   455→	if err != nil {
   456→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to get git author: %v\n", err)
   457→		return nil
   458→	}
   459→
   460→	// Get the active strategy
   461→	strat := GetStrategy()
   462→
   463→	// Get the session ID from the transcript path or input, then transform to Entire session ID
   464→	sessionID := input.SessionID
   465→	if sessionID == "" {
   466→		sessionID = paths.ExtractSessionIDFromTranscriptPath(input.TranscriptPath)
   467→	}
   468→
   469→	// Get next checkpoint sequence
   470→	seq := GetNextCheckpointSequence(sessionID, taskToolUseID)
   471→
   472→	// Extract the todo content from the tool_input.
   473→	// PostToolUse receives the NEW todo list where the just-completed work is
   474→	// marked as "completed". The last completed item is the work that was just done.
   475→	todoContent := ExtractLastCompletedTodoFromToolInput(input.ToolInput)
   476→	if todoContent == "" {
   477→		// No completed items - this is likely the first TodoWrite (planning phase).
   478→		// Check if there are any todos at all to avoid duplicate messages.
   479→		todoCount := CountTodosFromToolInput(input.ToolInput)
   480→		if todoCount > 0 {
   481→			// Use "Planning: N todos" format for the first TodoWrite
   482→			todoContent = fmt.Sprintf("Planning: %d todos", todoCount)
   483→		}
   484→		// If todoCount == 0, todoContent remains empty and FormatIncrementalMessage
   485→		// will fall back to "Checkpoint #N" format
   486→	}
   487→
   488→	// Get agent type from the currently executing hook agent (authoritative source)
   489→	var agentType agent.AgentType
   490→	if hookAgent, agentErr := GetCurrentHookAgent(); agentErr == nil {
   491→		agentType = hookAgent.Type()
   492→	}
   493→
   494→	// Build incremental checkpoint context
   495→	ctx := strategy.TaskCheckpointContext{
   496→		SessionID:           sessionID,
   497→		ToolUseID:           taskToolUseID,
   498→		ModifiedFiles:       changes.Modified,
   499→		NewFiles:            changes.New,
   500→		DeletedFiles:        changes.Deleted,
   501→		TranscriptPath:      input.TranscriptPath,
   502→		AuthorName:          author.Name,
   503→		AuthorEmail:         author.Email,
   504→		IsIncremental:       true,
   505→		IncrementalSequence: seq,
   506→		IncrementalType:     input.ToolName,
   507→		IncrementalData:     input.ToolInput,
   508→		TodoContent:         todoContent,
   509→		AgentType:           agentType,
   510→	}
   511→
   512→	// Save incremental checkpoint
   513→	if err := strat.SaveTaskCheckpoint(ctx); err != nil {
   514→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to save incremental checkpoint: %v\n", err)
   515→		return nil
   516→	}
   517→
   518→	fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "[entire] Created incremental checkpoint #%d for %s (task: %s)\n",
   519→		seq, input.ToolName, taskToolUseID[:min(12, len(taskToolUseID))])
   520→	return nil
   521→}
   522→
   523→// handleClaudeCodePreTask handles the PreToolUse[Task] hook
   524→func handleClaudeCodePreTask() error {
   525→	input, err := parseTaskHookInput(os.Stdin)
   526→	if err != nil {
   527→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse PreToolUse[Task] input: %w", err)
   528→	}
   529→
   530→	// Get agent for logging context
   531→	ag, err := GetCurrentHookAgent()
   532→	if err != nil {
   533→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", err)
   534→	}
   535→
   536→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
   537→	logging.Info(logCtx, "pre-task",
   538→		slog.String("hook", "pre-task"),
   539→		slog.String("hook_type", "subagent"),
   540→		slog.String("model_session_id", input.SessionID),
   541→		slog.String("transcript_path", input.TranscriptPath),
   542→		slog.String("tool_use_id", input.ToolUseID),
   543→	)
   544→
   545→	// Log context to stdout
   546→	logPreTaskHookContext(os.Stdout, input)
   547→
   548→	// Capture pre-task state locally (for computing new files when task completes).
   549→	// We don't create a shadow branch commit here. Commits are created during
   550→	// task completion (handleClaudeCodePostTask/handleClaudeCodePostTodo) only if the task resulted
   551→	// in file changes.
   552→	if err := CapturePreTaskState(input.ToolUseID); err != nil {
   553→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to capture pre-task state: %w", err)
   554→	}
   555→
   556→	return nil
   557→}
   558→
   559→// handleClaudeCodePostTask handles the PostToolUse[Task] hook
   560→func handleClaudeCodePostTask() error {
   561→	input, err := parsePostTaskHookInput(os.Stdin)
   562→	if err != nil {
   563→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse PostToolUse[Task] input: %w", err)
   564→	}
   565→
   566→	// Extract subagent type from tool_input for logging
   567→	subagentType, taskDescription := ParseSubagentTypeAndDescription(input.ToolInput)
   568→
   569→	// Get agent for logging context
   570→	ag, err := GetCurrentHookAgent()
   571→	if err != nil {
   572→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", err)
   573→	}
   574→
   575→	// Log parsed input context
   576→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
   577→	logging.Info(logCtx, "post-task",
   578→		slog.String("hook", "post-task"),
   579→		slog.String("hook_type", "subagent"),
   580→		slog.String("tool_use_id", input.ToolUseID),
   581→		slog.String("agent_id", input.AgentID),
   582→		slog.String("subagent_type", subagentType),
   583→	)
   584→
   585→	// Determine subagent transcript path
   586→	transcriptDir := filepath.Dir(input.TranscriptPath)
   587→	var subagentTranscriptPath string
   588→	if input.AgentID != "" {
   589→		subagentTranscriptPath = AgentTranscriptPath(transcriptDir, input.AgentID)
   590→		if !fileExists(subagentTranscriptPath) {
   591→			subagentTranscriptPath = ""
   592→		}
   593→	}
   594→
   595→	// Log context to stdout
   596→	logPostTaskHookContext(os.Stdout, input, subagentTranscriptPath)
   597→
   598→	// Parse transcript to extract modified files
   599→	var modifiedFiles []string
   600→	if subagentTranscriptPath != "" {
   601→		// Use subagent transcript if available
   602→		parsed, _, parseErr := transcript.ParseFromFileAtLine(subagentTranscriptPath, 0)
   603→		if parseErr != nil {
   604→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to parse subagent transcript: %v\n", parseErr)
   605→		} else {
   606→			modifiedFiles = extractModifiedFiles(parsed)
   607→		}
   608→	} else {
   609→		// Fall back to main transcript
   610→		parsed, _, parseErr := transcript.ParseFromFileAtLine(input.TranscriptPath, 0)
   611→		if parseErr != nil {
   612→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to parse transcript: %v\n", parseErr)
   613→		} else {
   614→			modifiedFiles = extractModifiedFiles(parsed)
   615→		}
   616→	}
   617→
   618→	// Load pre-task state and compute new files
   619→	preState, err := LoadPreTaskState(input.ToolUseID)
   620→	if err != nil {
   621→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load pre-task state: %v\n", err)
   622→	}
   623→
   624→	// Compute new and deleted files (single git status call)
   625→	changes, err := DetectFileChanges(preState.PreUntrackedFiles())
   626→	if err != nil {
   627→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to compute file changes: %v\n", err)
   628→	}
   629→
   630→	// Get repo root for path conversion (not cwd, since Claude may be in a subdirectory)
   631→	// Using cwd would filter out files in sibling directories (paths starting with ..)
   632→	repoRoot, err := paths.RepoRoot()
   633→	if err != nil {
   634→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get repo root: %w", err)
   635→	}
   636→
   637→	// Filter and normalize paths
   638→	relModifiedFiles := FilterAndNormalizePaths(modifiedFiles, repoRoot)
   639→	var relNewFiles, relDeletedFiles []string
   640→	if changes != nil {
   641→		relNewFiles = FilterAndNormalizePaths(changes.New, repoRoot)
   642→		relDeletedFiles = FilterAndNormalizePaths(changes.Deleted, repoRoot)
   643→	}
   644→
   645→	// If no file changes, skip creating a checkpoint
   646→	if len(relModifiedFiles) == 0 && len(relNewFiles) == 0 && len(relDeletedFiles) == 0 {
   647→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "[entire] No file changes detected, skipping task checkpoint\n")
   648→		// Cleanup pre-task state (ignore error - cleanup is best-effort)
   649→		_ = CleanupPreTaskState(input.ToolUseID) //nolint:errcheck // best-effort cleanup
   650→		return nil
   651→	}
   652→
   653→	// Find checkpoint UUID from main transcript (best-effort, ignore errors)
   654→	mainLines, _, _ := transcript.ParseFromFileAtLine(input.TranscriptPath, 0) //nolint:errcheck // best-effort extraction
   655→	checkpointUUID, _ := FindCheckpointUUID(mainLines, input.ToolUseID)
   656→
   657→	// Get git author
   658→	author, err := GetGitAuthor()
   659→	if err != nil {
   660→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get git author: %w", err)
   661→	}
   662→
   663→	// Get the configured strategy
   664→	strat := GetStrategy()
   665→
   666→	// Get agent type from the currently executing hook agent (authoritative source)
   667→	var agentType agent.AgentType
   668→	if hookAgent, agentErr := GetCurrentHookAgent(); agentErr == nil {
   669→		agentType = hookAgent.Type()
   670→	}
   671→
   672→	// Build task checkpoint context - strategy handles metadata creation
   673→	// Note: Incremental checkpoints are now created during task execution via handleClaudeCodePostTodo,
   674→	// so we don't need to collect/cleanup staging area here.
   675→	ctx := strategy.TaskCheckpointContext{
   676→		SessionID:              input.SessionID,
   677→		ToolUseID:              input.ToolUseID,
   678→		AgentID:                input.AgentID,
   679→		ModifiedFiles:          relModifiedFiles,
   680→		NewFiles:               relNewFiles,
   681→		DeletedFiles:           relDeletedFiles,
   682→		TranscriptPath:         input.TranscriptPath,
   683→		SubagentTranscriptPath: subagentTranscriptPath,
   684→		CheckpointUUID:         checkpointUUID,
   685→		AuthorName:             author.Name,
   686→		AuthorEmail:            author.Email,
   687→		SubagentType:           subagentType,
   688→		TaskDescription:        taskDescription,
   689→		AgentType:              agentType,
   690→	}
   691→
   692→	// Call strategy to save task checkpoint - strategy handles all metadata creation
   693→	if err := strat.SaveTaskCheckpoint(ctx); err != nil {
   694→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save task checkpoint: %w", err)
   695→	}
   696→
   697→	// Cleanup pre-task state (ignore error - cleanup is best-effort)
   698→	_ = CleanupPreTaskState(input.ToolUseID) //nolint:errcheck // best-effort cleanup
   699→
   700→	return nil
   701→}
   702→
   703→// handleClaudeCodeSessionStart handles the SessionStart hook for Claude Code.
   704→func handleClaudeCodeSessionStart() error {
   705→	return handleSessionStartCommon()
   706→}
   707→
   708→// handleClaudeCodeSessionEnd handles the SessionEnd hook for Claude Code.
   709→// This fires when the user explicitly closes the session.
   710→// Updates the session state with EndedAt timestamp.
   711→func handleClaudeCodeSessionEnd() error {
   712→	ag, err := GetCurrentHookAgent()
   713→	if err != nil {
   714→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get agent: %w", err)
   715→	}
   716→
   717→	input, err := ag.ParseHookInput(agent.HookSessionEnd, os.Stdin)
   718→	if err != nil {
   719→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to parse hook input: %w", err)
   720→	}
   721→
   722→	logCtx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), ag.Name())
   723→	logging.Info(logCtx, "session-end",
   724→		slog.String("hook", "session-end"),
   725→		slog.String("hook_type", "agent"),
   726→		slog.String("model_session_id", input.SessionID),
   727→	)
   728→
   729→	if input.SessionID == "" {
   730→		return nil // No session to update
   731→	}
   732→
   733→	// Best-effort cleanup - don't block session closure on failure
   734→	if err := markSessionEnded(input.SessionID); err != nil {
   735→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to mark session ended: %v\n", err)
   736→	}
   737→	return nil
   738→}
   739→
   740→// transitionSessionTurnEnd fires EventTurnEnd to move the session from
   741→// ACTIVE → IDLE. Best-effort: logs warnings on failure rather than
   742→// returning errors.
   743→func transitionSessionTurnEnd(sessionID string) {
   744→	turnState, loadErr := strategy.LoadSessionState(sessionID)
   745→	if loadErr != nil {
   746→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to load session state for turn end: %v\n", loadErr)
   747→		return
   748→	}
   749→	if turnState == nil {
   750→		return
   751→	}
   752→	if err := strategy.TransitionAndLog(turnState, session.EventTurnEnd, session.TransitionContext{}, session.NoOpActionHandler{}); err != nil {
   753→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: turn-end transition failed: %v\n", err)
   754→	}
   755→
   756→	// Always dispatch to strategy for turn-end handling. The strategy reads
   757→	// work items from state (e.g. TurnCheckpointIDs), not the action list.
   758→	strat := GetStrategy()
   759→	if handler, ok := strat.(strategy.TurnEndHandler); ok {
   760→		if err := handler.HandleTurnEnd(turnState); err != nil {
   761→			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: turn-end action dispatch failed: %v\n", err)
   762→		}
   763→	}
   764→
   765→	if updateErr := strategy.SaveSessionState(turnState); updateErr != nil {
   766→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to update session phase on turn end: %v\n", updateErr)
   767→	}
   768→}
   769→
   770→// markSessionEnded transitions the session to ENDED phase via the state machine.
   771→func markSessionEnded(sessionID string) error {
   772→	state, err := strategy.LoadSessionState(sessionID)
   773→	if err != nil {
   774→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to load session state: %w", err)
   775→	}
   776→	if state == nil {
   777→		return nil // No state file, nothing to update
   778→	}
   779→
   780→	if transErr := strategy.TransitionAndLog(state, session.EventSessionStop, session.TransitionContext{}, session.NoOpActionHandler{}); transErr != nil {
   781→		fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: session stop transition failed: %v\n", transErr)
   782→	}
   783→
   784→	now := time.Now()
   785→	state.EndedAt = &now
   786→
   787→	if err := strategy.SaveSessionState(state); err != nil {
   788→		return fmt.Errorf("failed to save session state: %w", err)
   789→	}
   790→	return nil
   791→}
   792→
   793→// stopHookSentinel is the string that appears in Claude Code's hook_progress
   794→// transcript entry when it launches our stop hook. Used to detect that the
   795→// transcript file has been fully flushed before we copy it.
   796→const stopHookSentinel = "hooks claude-code stop"
   797→
   798→// waitForTranscriptFlush polls the transcript file tail for the hook_progress
   799→// sentinel entry that Claude Code writes when launching the stop hook.
   800→// Once this entry appears in the file, all prior entries (assistant replies,
   801→// tool results) are guaranteed to have been flushed.
   802→//
   803→// hookStartTime is the approximate time our process started, used to avoid
   804→// matching stale sentinel entries from previous stop hook invocations.
   805→//
   806→// Falls back silently after a timeout — the transcript copy will proceed
   807→// with whatever data is available.
   808→func waitForTranscriptFlush(transcriptPath string, hookStartTime time.Time) {
   809→	const (
   810→		maxWait      = 3 * time.Second
   811→		pollInterval = 50 * time.Millisecond
   812→		tailBytes    = 4096 // Read last 4KB — sentinel is near the end
   813→		maxSkew      = 2 * time.Second
   814→	)
   815→
   816→	logCtx := logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks")
   817→	deadline := time.Now().Add(maxWait)
   818→	for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
   819→		if checkStopSentinel(transcriptPath, tailBytes, hookStartTime, maxSkew) {
   820→			logging.Debug(logCtx, "transcript flush sentinel found",
   821→				slog.Duration("wait", time.Since(hookStartTime)),
   822→			)
   823→			return
   824→		}
   825→		time.Sleep(pollInterval)
   826→	}
   827→	// Timeout — proceed with whatever is on disk.
   828→	logging.Warn(logCtx, "transcript flush sentinel not found within timeout, proceeding",
   829→		slog.Duration("timeout", maxWait),
   830→	)
   831→}
   832→
   833→// checkStopSentinel reads the tail of the transcript file and looks for a
   834→// hook_progress entry containing the stop hook sentinel, with a timestamp
   835→// close to hookStartTime.
   836→func checkStopSentinel(path string, tailBytes int64, hookStartTime time.Time, maxSkew time.Duration) bool {
   837→	f, err := os.Open(path) //nolint:gosec // path comes from agent hook input
   838→	if err != nil {
   839→		return false
   840→	}
   841→	defer f.Close()
   842→
   843→	// Seek to tail
   844→	info, err := f.Stat()
   845→	if err != nil {
   846→		return false
   847→	}
   848→	offset := info.Size() - tailBytes
   849→	if offset < 0 {
   850→		offset = 0
   851→	}
   852→	buf := make([]byte, info.Size()-offset)
   853→	if _, err := f.ReadAt(buf, offset); err != nil {
   854→		return false
   855→	}
   856→
   857→	// Scan lines from the tail for the sentinel
   858→	lines := strings.Split(string(buf), "\n")
   859→	for _, line := range lines {
   860→		line = strings.TrimSpace(line)
   861→		if line == "" || !strings.Contains(line, stopHookSentinel) {
   862→			continue
   863→		}
   864→
   865→		// Parse timestamp to check recency
   866→		var entry struct {
   867→			Timestamp string `json:"timestamp"`
   868→		}
   869→		if json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry) != nil || entry.Timestamp == "" {
   870→			continue
   871→		}
   872→		ts, err := time.Parse(time.RFC3339Nano, entry.Timestamp)
   873→		if err != nil {
   874→			ts, err = time.Parse(time.RFC3339, entry.Timestamp)
   875→			if err != nil {
   876→				continue
   877→			}
   878→		}
   879→
   880→		// Check timestamp is within skew window of our start time
   881→		if ts.After(hookStartTime.Add(-maxSkew)) && ts.Before(hookStartTime.Add(maxSkew)) {
   882→			return true
   883→		}
   884→	}
   885→	return false
   886→}
   887→

    19→// HookHandlerFunc is a function that handles a specific hook event.
    20→type HookHandlerFunc func() error
    21→
    22→// hookRegistry maps (agentName, hookName) to handler functions.
    23→// This allows agents to define their hook vocabulary while keeping
    24→// handler logic in the CLI package (avoiding circular dependencies).
    25→var hookRegistry = map[agent.AgentName]map[string]HookHandlerFunc{}
    26→
    27→// RegisterHookHandler registers a handler for an agent's hook.
    28→func RegisterHookHandler(agentName agent.AgentName, hookName string, handler HookHandlerFunc) {
    29→	if hookRegistry[agentName] == nil {
    30→		hookRegistry[agentName] = make(map[string]HookHandlerFunc)
    31→	}
    32→	hookRegistry[agentName][hookName] = handler
    33→}
    34→
    35→// GetHookHandler returns the handler for an agent's hook, or nil if not found.
    36→func GetHookHandler(agentName agent.AgentName, hookName string) HookHandlerFunc {
    37→	if handlers, ok := hookRegistry[agentName]; ok {
    38→		return handlers[hookName]
    39→	}
    40→	return nil
    41→}

   195→// agentHookLogCleanup stores the cleanup function for agent hook logging.
   196→// Set by PersistentPreRunE, called by PersistentPostRunE.
   197→var agentHookLogCleanup func()
   198→
   199→// currentHookAgentName stores the agent name for the currently executing hook.
   200→// Set by newAgentHookVerbCmdWithLogging before calling the handler.
   201→// This allows handlers to know which agent invoked the hook without guessing.
   202→var currentHookAgentName agent.AgentName
   203→
   204→// GetCurrentHookAgent returns the agent for the currently executing hook.
   205→// Returns the agent based on the hook command structure (e.g., "entire hooks claude-code ...")
   206→// rather than guessing from directory presence.
   207→// Falls back to GetAgent() if not in a hook context.
   208→//
   209→
   210→func GetCurrentHookAgent() (agent.Agent, error) {
   211→	if currentHookAgentName == "" {
   212→		return nil, errors.New("not in a hook context: agent name not set")
   213→	}
   214→
   215→	ag, err := agent.Get(currentHookAgentName)
   216→	if err != nil {
   217→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("getting hook agent %q: %w", currentHookAgentName, err)
   218→	}
   219→	return ag, nil
   220→}
   221→
   222→// newAgentHooksCmd creates a hooks subcommand for an agent that implements HookHandler.
   223→// It dynamically creates subcommands for each hook the agent supports.
   224→func newAgentHooksCmd(agentName agent.AgentName, handler agent.HookHandler) *cobra.Command {
   225→	cmd := &cobra.Command{
   226→		Use:    string(agentName),
   227→		Short:  handler.Description() + " hook handlers",
   228→		Hidden: true,
   229→		PersistentPreRunE: func(_ *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
   230→			agentHookLogCleanup = initHookLogging()
   231→			return nil
   232→		},
   233→		PersistentPostRunE: func(_ *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
   234→			if agentHookLogCleanup != nil {
   235→				agentHookLogCleanup()
   236→			}
   237→			return nil
   238→		},
   239→	}
   240→
   241→	for _, hookName := range handler.GetHookNames() {
   242→		cmd.AddCommand(newAgentHookVerbCmdWithLogging(agentName, hookName))
   243→	}
   244→
   245→	return cmd
   246→}
   247→
   248→// getHookType returns the hook type based on the hook name.
   249→// Returns "subagent" for task-related hooks (pre-task, post-task, post-todo),
   250→// "tool" for tool-related hooks (before-tool, after-tool),
   251→// "agent" for all other agent hooks.
   252→func getHookType(hookName string) string {
   253→	switch hookName {
   254→	case claudecode.HookNamePreTask, claudecode.HookNamePostTask, claudecode.HookNamePostTodo:
   255→		return "subagent"
   256→	case geminicli.HookNameBeforeTool, geminicli.HookNameAfterTool:
   257→		return "tool"
   258→	default:
   259→		return "agent"
   260→	}
   261→}
   262→
   263→// newAgentHookVerbCmdWithLogging creates a command for a specific hook verb with structured logging.
   264→// It logs hook invocation at DEBUG level and completion with duration at INFO level.
   265→func newAgentHookVerbCmdWithLogging(agentName agent.AgentName, hookName string) *cobra.Command {
   266→	return &cobra.Command{
   267→		Use:    hookName,
   268→		Hidden: true,
   269→		Short:  "Called on " + hookName,
   270→		RunE: func(_ *cobra.Command, _ []string) error {
   271→			// Skip silently if not in a git repository - hooks shouldn't prevent the agent from working
   272→			if _, err := paths.RepoRoot(); err != nil {
   273→				return nil
   274→			}
   275→
   276→			start := time.Now()
   277→
   278→			// Initialize logging context with agent name
   279→			ctx := logging.WithAgent(logging.WithComponent(context.Background(), "hooks"), agentName)
   280→
   281→			// Get strategy name for logging
   282→			strategyName := unknownStrategyName //nolint:ineffassign,wastedassign // already present in codebase
   283→			strategyName = GetStrategy().Name()
   284→
   285→			hookType := getHookType(hookName)
   286→
   287→			logging.Debug(ctx, "hook invoked",
   288→				slog.String("hook", hookName),
   289→				slog.String("hook_type", hookType),
   290→				slog.String("strategy", strategyName),
   291→			)
   292→
   293→			handler := GetHookHandler(agentName, hookName)
   294→			if handler == nil {
   295→				logging.Error(ctx, "no handler registered",
   296→					slog.String("hook", hookName),
   297→					slog.String("hook_type", hookType),
   298→				)
   299→				return fmt.Errorf("no handler registered for %s/%s", agentName, hookName)
   300→			}
   301→
   302→			// Set the current hook agent so handlers can retrieve it
   303→			// without guessing from directory presence
   304→			currentHookAgentName = agentName
   305→			defer func() { currentHookAgentName = "" }()
   306→
   307→			hookErr := handler()
   308→
   309→			logging.LogDuration(ctx, slog.LevelDebug, "hook completed", start,
   310→				slog.String("hook", hookName),
   311→				slog.String("hook_type", hookType),
   312→				slog.String("strategy", strategyName),
   313→				slog.Bool("success", hookErr == nil),
   314→			)
   315→
   316→			return hookErr
   317→		},
   318→	}
   319→}

content.thinking:
The transcript offset is off by one. The issue is that `ClaudeCodeAgent.GetTranscriptPosition()` counts lines differently than the CLI-level `GetTranscriptPosition()`.

Let me check: the test content has 3 lines (separated by newlines) for the first test and 2 lines for the second. But `ClaudeCodeAgent.GetTranscriptPosition()` is returning 2 and 1 respectively.

The issue is that the test transcript doesn't have a trailing newline. The `ClaudeCodeAgent.GetTranscriptPosition()` counts only lines that end with `\n`. In JSONL format, the last line may not have a trailing newline.


Looking at the implementation, it's reading bytes until it hits a newline character, incrementing the count for each complete line. If the file ends without a trailing newline, that final line never gets counted because the loop breaks on EOF before incrementing. I see the core issue now—the agent version breaks immediately on EOF without counting that final line, whereas the CLI version increments the counter first and then breaks on the next iteration when it gets empty bytes. The agent's logic skips the last line entirely if there's no trailing newline, while the CLI correctly accounts for it. I'm realizing the simplest fix is to add trailing newlines to the test transcript data, since production JSONL files always end with a newline anyway. Let me update the test to match that behavior.

content.text: The test is failing because `ClaudeCodeAgent.GetTranscriptPosition()` doesn't count the last line when there's no trailing newline. Let me check and fix the agent implementation.

   - **2a**: Unify PrePromptState capture (merge `CapturePrePromptState` and `CaptureGeminiPrePromptState`)

   - **TranscriptAnalyzer optional interface**: Provides `GetTranscriptPosition()`, `ExtractModifiedFilesFromOffset()`, `ExtractPrompts()`, `ExtractSummary()`
   - **PrePromptState unification**: Merging Claude's `StepTranscriptStart` (JSONL line count) and Gemini's `StartMessageIndex` (JSON message count) into a single `TranscriptOffset int` field
   - **Backward compatibility**: `normalizePrePromptState()` migrates deprecated JSON fields on load

       ```go
       type PrePromptState struct {

           UntrackedFiles []string `json:"untracked_files"`

           // Deprecated fields kept for backward compat:
           StartMessageIndex       int    `json:"start_message_index,omitempty"`
       ```

     - **normalizePrePromptState()** — updated to migrate all deprecated fields to TranscriptOffset:
       ```go
       func (s *PrePromptState) normalizePrePromptState() {
           if s.StepTranscriptStart == 0 && s.LastTranscriptLineCount > 0 {
               s.StepTranscriptStart = s.LastTranscriptLineCount
           }
           s.LastTranscriptLineCount = 0
           if s.TranscriptOffset == 0 {
               if s.StepTranscriptStart > 0 {
                   s.TranscriptOffset = s.StepTranscriptStart
               } else if s.StartMessageIndex > 0 {
                   s.TranscriptOffset = s.StartMessageIndex
               }
           }
           s.StepTranscriptStart = 0
           s.StartMessageIndex = 0
       }
       ```
     - **CapturePrePromptState** — signature changed from `(sessionID, transcriptPath string)` to `(ag agent.Agent, sessionID, sessionRef string)`, now uses `TranscriptAnalyzer.GetTranscriptPosition()`:
       ```go
       func CapturePrePromptState(ag agent.Agent, sessionID, sessionRef string) error {
           // ... setup (tmp dir, untracked files) ...
           var transcriptOffset int
           if analyzer, ok := ag.(agent.TranscriptAnalyzer); ok && sessionRef != "" {
               pos, posErr := analyzer.GetTranscriptPosition(sessionRef)
               if posErr != nil {
                   fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Warning: failed to get transcript position: %v\n", posErr)
               } else {
                   transcriptOffset = pos
               }
           }
           state := PrePromptState{
               SessionID:        sessionID,
               Timestamp:        time.Now().UTC().Format(time.RFC3339),
       ```
